name: NetAlertX
description: NetAlertX is a network visibility and asset intelligence tool that discovers devices via ARP and passive protocols. This script deploys the official Docker image in an LXC with host networking for accurate scanning.

notes.text: Defaults to a privileged LXC with nesting and keyctl so Docker can use host networking and ARP/raw sockets. Unprivileged mode often limits discovery: `var_unprivileged=1 bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oraad/proxmox-scripts/main/ct/netalertx.sh)"`.
Host networking is mandatory for the NetAlertX container (ARP scanning and passive discovery). The LXC should be on the same LAN/VLAN as the networks you want to monitor.
Web UI: http://<container-ip>:20211 (GraphQL API on 20212). Initial discovery can take 5–10 minutes.

Optional SCAN_SUBNETS at install: enter a CIDR and interface (e.g. 192.168.1.0/24 and eth0), or leave empty and set SCAN_SUBNETS in Settings after first start. Unattended: `var_scan_subnets="['192.168.1.0/24 --interface=eth0']" bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oraad/proxmox-scripts/main/ct/netalertx.sh)"`.
Upstream compose sysctls (arp_ignore / arp_announce) are omitted because they often fail inside LXC. Set them on the Proxmox host if you need ARP flux mitigation.

Update an existing container: run `update` inside the LXC, or `pct exec <CTID> -- update` from the Proxmox host. Interactive update shows Silent/Verbose/Cancel, then Update NetAlertX or Remove Unused Images. Unattended (`PHS_SILENT=1` or non-TTY) runs a silent app update.
Data lives under /opt/netalertx/data (config/ and db/). Official image: ghcr.io/netalertx/netalertx:latest.

Alpine template: run `pveam update`, then `pveam available --section system | grep alpine-3.23`. Download if missing: `pveam download local alpine-3.23-default_20260116_amd64.tar.xz` (use the exact name from `pveam available` on your arch). Fallback: `var_version=3.22` if 3.23 is not listed on an older Proxmox host.
